curriculum.subject: Chemistry
curriculum.overallDescription: 11 units, 9 chapters, 71 topics, 10 subtopics, and 25 learning outcomes extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.
coreObjectives: Apply calorimetry techniques to measure enthalpy changes (∆U and ∆H)
Distinguish between different types of reaction enthalpies
Predict the spontaneity of reactions using Gibbs Energy Change
Relate equilibrium constants to reaction quotients and Gibbs energy
Explain dynamic equilibrium in physical and chemical processes
Apply the Law of Chemical Equilibrium to determine equilibrium constants
Predict shifts in equilibrium based on concentration, temperature, and pressure changes
Calculate pH and buffer capacity in ionic solutions
Classify reactions based on classical and electron transfer definitions of redox
Determine oxidation numbers for elements in compounds
Analyze electrode processes in redox reactions

practicals.title: Cutting glass tube and glass rod
practicals.details: Basic laboratory skill involving cutting glass tubes and rods safely.

practicals.title: Bending a glass tube
practicals.details: Technique for bending glass tubes to specific shapes for laboratory use.

practicals.title: Drawing out a glass jet
practicals.details: Process of drawing out a glass tube to create a fine jet for experiments.

practicals.title: Boring a cork
practicals.details: Skill in creating holes in corks to fit glass tubes or thermometers.

practicals.title: Determination of melting point of an organic compound
practicals.unit_name: Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances
practicals.details: Experimental determination of the melting point to characterize an organic substance.

practicals.title: Determination of boiling point of an organic compound
practicals.unit_name: Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances
practicals.details: Experimental determination of the boiling point to characterize an organic substance.

practicals.title: Crystallization of impure sample (Alum, Copper Sulphate, or Benzoic Acid)
practicals.unit_name: Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances
practicals.details: Purification technique involving crystallization of specific impure samples.

practicals.title: Determination of pH using pH paper or universal indicator
practicals.details: Determining the pH of fruit juices, acids, bases, and salts using indicators.

practicals.title: Comparing pH of strong and weak acids
practicals.details: Comparative study of pH levels in solutions of strong and weak acids of same concentration.

practicals.title: Study the pH change in titration using universal indicator
practicals.details: Observing pH changes during the titration of a strong base.

practicals.title: Study the pH change by common-ion in weak acids and bases
practicals.details: Investigation into the effect of common ions on the pH of weak acid and base solutions.

practicals.title: Study shift in equilibrium between ferric ions and thiocyanate ions
practicals.details: Observing the shift in equilibrium by varying the concentration of either ion.

practicals.title: Study shift in equilibrium between [Co(H2O)6] and chloride ions
practicals.details: Observing the shift in equilibrium by changing the concentration of either ion.

practicals.title: Using mechanical/electronic balance for Quantitative Estimation
practicals.details: Fundamental skill in using balances for accurate mass measurement.

practicals.title: Preparation of standard solution of Oxalic acid
practicals.details: Creating a standard solution of oxalic acid for titration purposes.

practicals.title: Determination of strength of Sodium hydroxide by titration with Oxalic acid
practicals.details: Volumetric analysis to find the concentration of a given NaOH solution.

practicals.title: Preparation of standard solution of Sodium carbonate
practicals.details: Creating a standard solution of sodium carbonate for titration.

practicals.title: Determination of strength of Hydrochloric acid by titration with Sodium Carbonate
practicals.details: Volumetric analysis to find the concentration of a given HCl solution.

practicals.title: Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt
practicals.details: Identification of specific cations (e.g., Pb, Cu, Al, Fe, Mn, Ni, Zn, Co, Ca, Sr, Ba, Mg, NH4) and anions (e.g., CO3, S, SO3, NO3, NO2, Cl, Br, I, SO4, PO4, CH3COO) in a salt sample.

practicals.title: Detection of Nitrogen, Sulphur, Chlorine in organic compounds
practicals.details: Chemical tests to detect the presence of nitrogen, sulphur, and chlorine in organic substances.

projects.title: Checking the bacterial contamination in drinking water by testing sulphide ion
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing to detect sulphide ions indicating bacterial contamination.

projects.title: Study of the methods of purification of water
projects.details: Investigation into various techniques used for purifying water sources.

projects.title: Testing the hardness, presence of Iron, Fluoride, Chloride, etc., in drinking water
projects.details: Regional variation study of ions in drinking water and analysis of causes for presence above permissible limits.

projects.title: Investigation of the foaming capacity of different washing soaps
projects.details: Study on the effect of adding Sodium carbonate on the foaming capacity of various washing soaps.

projects.title: Study the acidity of different samples of tea leaves
projects.details: Analysis of acidity levels in various tea leaf samples.

projects.title: Determination of the rate of evaporation of different liquids
projects.details: Experimental study comparing evaporation rates across different liquid types.

projects.title: Study the effect of acids and bases on the tensile strength of fibers
projects.details: Investigation into how acidic and basic environments affect the structural integrity of fibers.

projects.title: Study of acidity of fruit and vegetable juices
projects.details: Analysis of pH levels in various fruit and vegetable juice samples.

marks_distribution: Unit 1: Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry - 7 Marks
Unit 3: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties - 6 Marks
Unit 4: Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure - 7 Marks
Unit 8: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques - 11 Marks
question_paper_design: Formative Assessment Topics (s & p Block Elements, The Gaseous State): Not included in summative assessment
competency_weightage: Atomic Models and Quantum Mechanical Model
Chemical Bonding Theories (Kossel-Lewis, VSEPR, Valence Bond, Molecular Orbital)
Equilibrium Constants and Factors Affecting Equilibria
bloom_levels: Knowledge: Laws of Chemical Combination, Atomic Theory, Periodic Law
Understanding: Bohr's Model, Bonding Theories, Thermodynamic Terms
Application: Stoichiometric Calculations, pH Determination, Titration
Analysis: Electronic Configuration, Equilibrium Shifts, Qualitative Analysis
Evaluation: Project Work and Scientific Investigations
internal_assessment: Formative Assessment for s & p Block Elements (Unit 10)
Formative Assessment for The Gaseous State (Unit 11)

syllabus_areas: Basic Laboratory Techniques (Cutting, Bending, Drawing, Boring)
Characterization and Purification (Melting/Boiling Point, Crystallization)
Experiments based on pH (Fruit juices, Strong/Weak acids, Titration)
Chemical Equilibrium (Ferric-Thiocyanate, Cobalt-Chloride)
Quantitative Estimation (Balance usage, Standard solutions, Titration)
Qualitative Analysis (Cations and Anions detection, Organic elements)

project_assessment.description: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources
suggested_topics: Checking bacterial contamination in drinking water
Methods of purification of water
Testing hardness and ions in regional drinking water
Foaming capacity of washing soaps
Acidity of tea leaves or fruit/vegetable juices
Rate of evaporation of different liquids
Effect of acids/bases on tensile strength of fibers

practicals.title: Using mechanical/electronic balance for Quantitative Estimation
practicals.details: Fundamental skill in using balances for accurate mass measurement.

practicals.title: Preparation of standard solution of Oxalic acid
practicals.details: Creating a standard solution of oxalic acid for titration purposes.

practicals.title: Determination of strength of Sodium hydroxide by titration with Oxalic acid
practicals.details: Volumetric analysis to find the concentration of a given NaOH solution.

practicals.title: Preparation of standard solution of Sodium carbonate
practicals.details: Creating a standard solution of sodium carbonate for titration.

practicals.title: Determination of strength of Hydrochloric acid by titration with Sodium Carbonate
practicals.details: Volumetric analysis to find the concentration of a given HCl solution.

practicals.title: Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt
practicals.details: Identification of specific cations (e.g., Pb, Cu, Al, Fe, Mn, Ni, Zn, Co, Ca, Sr, Ba, Mg, NH4) and anions (e.g., CO3, S, SO3, NO3, NO2, Cl, Br, I, SO4, PO4, CH3COO) in a salt sample.

practicals.title: Detection of Nitrogen, Sulphur, Chlorine in organic compounds
practicals.details: Chemical tests to detect the presence of nitrogen, sulphur, and chlorine in organic substances.

projects.title: Checking the bacterial contamination in drinking water by testing sulphide ion
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing to detect sulphide ions indicating bacterial contamination.

projects.title: Study of the methods of purification of water
projects.details: Investigation into various techniques used for purifying water sources.

projects.title: Testing the hardness, presence of Iron, Fluoride, Chloride, etc., in drinking water
projects.details: Regional variation study of ions in drinking water and analysis of causes for presence above permissible limits.

projects.title: Investigation of the foaming capacity of different washing soaps
projects.details: Study on the effect of adding Sodium carbonate on the foaming capacity of various washing soaps.

projects.title: Study the acidity of different samples of tea leaves
projects.details: Analysis of acidity levels in various tea leaf samples.

projects.title: Determination of the rate of evaporation of different liquids
projects.details: Experimental study comparing evaporation rates across different liquid types.

projects.title: Study the effect of acids and bases on the tensile strength of fibers
projects.details: Investigation into how acidic and basic environments affect the structural integrity of fibers.

projects.title: Study of acidity of fruit and vegetable juices
projects.details: Analysis of pH levels in various fruit and vegetable juice samples.

marks_distribution: Unit 1: Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry - 7 Marks
Unit 3: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties - 6 Marks
Unit 4: Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure - 7 Marks
Unit 8: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques - 11 Marks
question_paper_design: Formative Assessment Topics (s & p Block Elements, The Gaseous State): Not included in summative assessment
competency_weightage: Atomic Models and Quantum Mechanical Model
Chemical Bonding Theories (Kossel-Lewis, VSEPR, Valence Bond, Molecular Orbital)
Equilibrium Constants and Factors Affecting Equilibria
bloom_levels: Knowledge: Laws of Chemical Combination, Atomic Theory, Periodic Law
Understanding: Bohr's Model, Bonding Theories, Thermodynamic Terms
Application: Stoichiometric Calculations, pH Determination, Titration
Analysis: Electronic Configuration, Equilibrium Shifts, Qualitative Analysis
Evaluation: Project Work and Scientific Investigations
internal_assessment: Formative Assessment for s & p Block Elements (Unit 10)
Formative Assessment for The Gaseous State (Unit 11)

syllabus_areas: Basic Laboratory Techniques (Cutting, Bending, Drawing, Boring)
Characterization and Purification (Melting/Boiling Point, Crystallization)
Experiments based on pH (Fruit juices, Strong/Weak acids, Titration)
Chemical Equilibrium (Ferric-Thiocyanate, Cobalt-Chloride)
Quantitative Estimation (Balance usage, Standard solutions, Titration)
Qualitative Analysis (Cations and Anions detection, Organic elements)

project_assessment.description: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources

marks_distribution: Unit 1: Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry - 7 Marks
Unit 3: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties - 6 Marks
Unit 4: Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure - 7 Marks
Unit 8: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques - 11 Marks
question_paper_design: Formative Assessment Topics (s & p Block Elements, The Gaseous State): Not included in summative assessment
competency_weightage: Atomic Models and Quantum Mechanical Model
Chemical Bonding Theories (Kossel-Lewis, VSEPR, Valence Bond, Molecular Orbital)
Equilibrium Constants and Factors Affecting Equilibria
bloom_levels: Knowledge: Laws of Chemical Combination, Atomic Theory, Periodic Law
Understanding: Bohr's Model, Bonding Theories, Thermodynamic Terms
Application: Stoichiometric Calculations, pH Determination, Titration
Analysis: Electronic Configuration, Equilibrium Shifts, Qualitative Analysis
Evaluation: Project Work and Scientific Investigations
internal_assessment: Formative Assessment for s & p Block Elements (Unit 10)
Formative Assessment for The Gaseous State (Unit 11)

syllabus_areas: Basic Laboratory Techniques (Cutting, Bending, Drawing, Boring)
Characterization and Purification (Melting/Boiling Point, Crystallization)
Experiments based on pH (Fruit juices, Strong/Weak acids, Titration)
Chemical Equilibrium (Ferric-Thiocyanate, Cobalt-Chloride)
Quantitative Estimation (Balance usage, Standard solutions, Titration)
Qualitative Analysis (Cations and Anions detection, Organic elements)

project_assessment.description: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources

Methods of purification of water
Testing hardness and ions in regional drinking water
Foaming capacity of washing soaps
Acidity of tea leaves or fruit/vegetable juices
Rate of evaporation of different liquids
Effect of acids/bases on tensile strength of fibers

long_chapter_names_reclassified: Organic Chemistry – Some Basic Principles and Techniques

competency_groups.title: Thermodynamics and Energy Transformations
competency_groups.description: Ability to apply thermodynamic principles to analyze energy changes, predict reaction spontaneity, and understand equilibrium states in chemical systems.
competencies: Apply calorimetry techniques to measure enthalpy changes (∆U and ∆H)
Distinguish between different types of reaction enthalpies
Predict the spontaneity of reactions using Gibbs Energy Change
Relate equilibrium constants to reaction quotients and Gibbs energy

competency_groups.title: Chemical and Ionic Equilibrium Systems
competency_groups.description: Ability to analyze dynamic equilibrium in physical and chemical processes, calculate equilibrium constants, and predict shifts in response to external factors.
competencies: Explain dynamic equilibrium in physical and chemical processes
Apply the Law of Chemical Equilibrium to determine equilibrium constants
Analyze homogeneous and heterogeneous equilibria
Predict shifts in equilibrium based on concentration, temperature, and pressure changes
Calculate pH and buffer capacity in ionic solutions

competency_groups.title: Redox Processes and Electrode Dynamics
competency_groups.description: Ability to identify oxidation-reduction reactions, assign oxidation numbers, and understand electron transfer mechanisms in electrode processes.
competencies: Classify reactions based on classical and electron transfer definitions of redox
Determine oxidation numbers for elements in compounds
Analyze electrode processes in redox reactions

competency_groups.title: Organic Chemistry Principles and Techniques
competency_groups.description: Ability to understand the structural basis of organic compounds, apply nomenclature rules, analyze isomerism, and utilize purification and analysis techniques.
competencies: Represent organic structures using standard conventions
Apply IUPAC nomenclature rules to classify organic compounds
Identify and differentiate between various types of isomers
Analyze fundamental concepts in organic reaction mechanisms
Execute qualitative and quantitative analysis of organic compounds
competency_groups.unit_name: Organic Chemistry – Some Basic Principles and Techniques
competency_groups.chapter_name: Organic Chemistry – Some Basic Principles and Techniques

competency_groups.title: Hydrocarbon Classification and Properties
competency_groups.description: Ability to classify hydrocarbons, understand their structural characteristics, and evaluate their physical properties and toxicity.
competencies: Classify hydrocarbons into alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, and aromatic compounds
Correlate structure with physical properties of hydrocarbons
Evaluate the carcinogenic potential and toxicity of specific hydrocarbons

competency_groups.title: s & p Block Element Trends (Formative)
competency_groups.description: Ability to observe periodic trends in physical and chemical properties of s and p block elements, including electronic configurations and unique behaviors.
competencies: Relate electronic configuration to position in the periodic table
Analyze trends in atomic/ionic radii, ionization enthalpy, and hydration enthalpy
Identify unique behavior of the first element in each group

competency_groups.title: Gaseous State Behavior (Formative)
competency_groups.description: Ability to apply gas laws qualitatively and understand deviations from ideal behavior.
competencies: Apply qualitative gas laws to predict state changes
Analyze the ideal gas equation and its limitations

competency_groups.title: Practical Laboratory Skills and Safety
competency_groups.description: Ability to perform volumetric analysis, salt analysis, and content-based experiments with precision, adhering to safety protocols.
competencies: Perform volumetric analysis (titrations) accurately
Conduct qualitative analysis to identify cations and anions
Execute purification techniques such as crystallization
Determine physical constants like melting and boiling points

competency_groups.title: Scientific Investigation and Project Work
competency_groups.description: Ability to design and execute scientific investigations, collect data from multiple sources, and analyze real-world chemical phenomena.
competencies: Design experiments to test hypotheses regarding water quality or chemical properties
Analyze the effect of variables on chemical reactions (e.g., acidity, evaporation rate)
Synthesize findings from laboratory testing and external research

marks_distribution: Unit 1: Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry - 7 Marks
Unit 3: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties - 6 Marks
Unit 4: Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure - 7 Marks
Unit 8: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques - 11 Marks
question_paper_design: Formative Assessment Topics (s & p Block Elements, The Gaseous State): Not included in summative assessment
competency_weightage: Atomic Models and Quantum Mechanical Model
Chemical Bonding Theories (Kossel-Lewis, VSEPR, Valence Bond, Molecular Orbital)
Equilibrium Constants and Factors Affecting Equilibria
bloom_levels: Knowledge: Laws of Chemical Combination, Atomic Theory, Periodic Law
Understanding: Bohr's Model, Bonding Theories, Thermodynamic Terms
Application: Stoichiometric Calculations, pH Determination, Titration
Analysis: Electronic Configuration, Equilibrium Shifts, Qualitative Analysis
Evaluation: Project Work and Scientific Investigations
internal_assessment: Formative Assessment for s & p Block Elements (Unit 10)
Formative Assessment for The Gaseous State (Unit 11)

syllabus_areas: Basic Laboratory Techniques (Cutting, Bending, Drawing, Boring)
Characterization and Purification (Melting/Boiling Point, Crystallization)
Experiments based on pH (Fruit juices, Strong/Weak acids, Titration)
Chemical Equilibrium (Ferric-Thiocyanate, Cobalt-Chloride)
Quantitative Estimation (Balance usage, Standard solutions, Titration)
Qualitative Analysis (Cations and Anions detection, Organic elements)

project_assessment.description: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources
suggested_topics: Checking bacterial contamination in drinking water
Methods of purification of water
Testing hardness and ions in regional drinking water
Foaming capacity of washing soaps
Acidity of tea leaves or fruit/vegetable juices
Rate of evaporation of different liquids
Effect of acids/bases on tensile strength of fibers

outcomes: Apply calorimetry techniques to measure enthalpy changes (∆U and ∆H)
Distinguish between different types of reaction enthalpies
Predict the spontaneity of reactions using Gibbs Energy Change
Relate equilibrium constants to reaction quotients and Gibbs energy
Explain dynamic equilibrium in physical and chemical processes
Apply the Law of Chemical Equilibrium to determine equilibrium constants
Analyze homogeneous and heterogeneous equilibria
Predict shifts in equilibrium based on concentration, temperature, and pressure changes
Calculate pH and buffer capacity in ionic solutions
Classify reactions based on classical and electron transfer definitions of redox
Determine oxidation numbers for elements in compounds
Analyze electrode processes in redox reactions

projects.title: Checking the bacterial contamination in drinking water by testing sulphide ion
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing to detect sulphide ions indicating bacterial contamination.

projects.title: Study of the methods of purification of water
projects.details: Investigation into various techniques used for purifying water sources.

projects.title: Testing the hardness, presence of Iron, Fluoride, Chloride, etc., in drinking water
projects.details: Regional variation study of ions in drinking water and analysis of causes for presence above permissible limits.

projects.title: Investigation of the foaming capacity of different washing soaps
projects.details: Study on the effect of adding Sodium carbonate on the foaming capacity of various washing soaps.

projects.title: Study the acidity of different samples of tea leaves
projects.details: Analysis of acidity levels in various tea leaf samples.

projects.title: Determination of the rate of evaporation of different liquids
projects.details: Experimental study comparing evaporation rates across different liquid types.

projects.title: Study the effect of acids and bases on the tensile strength of fibers
projects.details: Investigation into how acidic and basic environments affect the structural integrity of fibers.

projects.title: Study of acidity of fruit and vegetable juices
projects.details: Analysis of pH levels in various fruit and vegetable juice samples.

practicals.title: Cutting glass tube and glass rod
practicals.details: Basic laboratory skill involving cutting glass tubes and rods safely.

practicals.title: Bending a glass tube
practicals.details: Technique for bending glass tubes to specific shapes for laboratory use.

practicals.title: Drawing out a glass jet
practicals.details: Process of drawing out a glass tube to create a fine jet for experiments.

practicals.title: Boring a cork
practicals.details: Skill in creating holes in corks to fit glass tubes or thermometers.

practicals.title: Determination of melting point of an organic compound
practicals.unit_name: Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances
practicals.details: Experimental determination of the melting point to characterize an organic substance.

practicals.title: Determination of boiling point of an organic compound
practicals.unit_name: Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances
practicals.details: Experimental determination of the boiling point to characterize an organic substance.

practicals.title: Crystallization of impure sample (Alum, Copper Sulphate, or Benzoic Acid)
practicals.unit_name: Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances
practicals.details: Purification technique involving crystallization of specific impure samples.

practicals.title: Determination of pH using pH paper or universal indicator
practicals.details: Determining the pH of fruit juices, acids, bases, and salts using indicators.

practicals.title: Comparing pH of strong and weak acids
practicals.details: Comparative study of pH levels in solutions of strong and weak acids of same concentration.

practicals.title: Study the pH change in titration using universal indicator
practicals.details: Observing pH changes during the titration of a strong base.

practicals.title: Study the pH change by common-ion in weak acids and bases
practicals.details: Investigation into the effect of common ions on the pH of weak acid and base solutions.

practicals.title: Study shift in equilibrium between ferric ions and thiocyanate ions
practicals.details: Observing the shift in equilibrium by varying the concentration of either ion.

practicals.title: Study shift in equilibrium between [Co(H2O)6] and chloride ions
practicals.details: Observing the shift in equilibrium by changing the concentration of either ion.

practicals.title: Using mechanical/electronic balance for Quantitative Estimation
practicals.details: Fundamental skill in using balances for accurate mass measurement.

practicals.title: Preparation of standard solution of Oxalic acid
practicals.details: Creating a standard solution of oxalic acid for titration purposes.

practicals.title: Determination of strength of Sodium hydroxide by titration with Oxalic acid
practicals.details: Volumetric analysis to find the concentration of a given NaOH solution.

practicals.title: Preparation of standard solution of Sodium carbonate
practicals.details: Creating a standard solution of sodium carbonate for titration.

practicals.title: Determination of strength of Hydrochloric acid by titration with Sodium Carbonate
practicals.details: Volumetric analysis to find the concentration of a given HCl solution.

practicals.title: Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt
practicals.details: Identification of specific cations (e.g., Pb, Cu, Al, Fe, Mn, Ni, Zn, Co, Ca, Sr, Ba, Mg, NH4) and anions (e.g., CO3, S, SO3, NO3, NO2, Cl, Br, I, SO4, PO4, CH3COO) in a salt sample.

practicals.title: Detection of Nitrogen, Sulphur, Chlorine in organic compounds
practicals.details: Chemical tests to detect the presence of nitrogen, sulphur, and chlorine in organic substances.

question_paper_design: Formative Assessment Topics (s & p Block Elements, The Gaseous State): Not included in summative assessment
competency_weightage: Atomic Models and Quantum Mechanical Model
Chemical Bonding Theories (Kossel-Lewis, VSEPR, Valence Bond, Molecular Orbital)
Equilibrium Constants and Factors Affecting Equilibria
bloom_levels: Knowledge: Laws of Chemical Combination, Atomic Theory, Periodic Law
Understanding: Bohr's Model, Bonding Theories, Thermodynamic Terms
Application: Stoichiometric Calculations, pH Determination, Titration
Analysis: Electronic Configuration, Equilibrium Shifts, Qualitative Analysis
Evaluation: Project Work and Scientific Investigations
internal_assessment: Formative Assessment for s & p Block Elements (Unit 10)
Formative Assessment for The Gaseous State (Unit 11)

Characterization and Purification (Melting/Boiling Point, Crystallization)
Experiments based on pH (Fruit juices, Strong/Weak acids, Titration)
Chemical Equilibrium (Ferric-Thiocyanate, Cobalt-Chloride)
Quantitative Estimation (Balance usage, Standard solutions, Titration)
Qualitative Analysis (Cations and Anions detection, Organic elements)
